name: Super Balade 4: Tour and Detours at Bordeaux Lake
name_original: Entre le lac de Bordeaux et la réserve du Barail
description: Super Balade 4: Tour and Detours at Bordeaux Lake is a moderate 6.2-mile hike exploring natural areas and urban sights.
region_intro: Easy hiking trails around Lac De Bordeaux are located approximately 6 km north of Bordeaux's city center. The area features a 1 km² artificial lake surrounded by woodlands, meadows, and urban green spaces. The terrain is generally flat with minimal elevation changes, making it suitable for accessible paths and easy strolls. This network of green spaces connects to natural reserves such as the Barail reserve and the Réserve naturelle des Marais de Bruges. Best easy hiking trails around Lac De Bordeaux The most popular easy hiking route is Bordeaux lake – Lac de Bordeaux footbridge loop from Stade Atlantique, a 4.1 miles (6.6 km) trail that takes 1 hour 39 minutes to complete. This route offers views of the lake and its surroundings. Another top favourite among local hikers is Beach of Lake Bordeaux – Bordeaux lake loop from Réserve naturelle des Marais de Bruges, an easy 4.8 miles (7.8 km) path. This trail winds through lakeside areas and green spaces, with an average completion time of 1 hour 57 minutes. Local hikers also love the Bordeaux lake – Beach of Lake Bordeaux loop from Le Bouscat, a 3.4 miles (5.4 km) trail leading through parkland and along the lake shore, often completed in about 1 hour 22 minutes. Hiking around Lac De Bordeaux is defined by its artificial lake, surrounding woodlands, and interconnected green spaces. The network offers options for different ability levels, with over 300 easy hiking trails available. The routes in Lac De Bordeaux are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.4 stars from nearly 700 reviews. More than 6,700 hikers have used komoot to explore Lac De Bordeaux's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many easy hiking trails are available around Lac de Bordeaux?
A: There are over 300 easy hiking trails around Lac de Bordeaux, offering a wide variety of options for all skill levels. The area is known for its generally flat terrain and minimal elevation changes, making it ideal for leisurely walks.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on easy hikes near Lac de Bordeaux?
A: The terrain around Lac de Bordeaux is predominantly flat with minimal elevation changes. You'll find a mix of paths through woodlands, open meadows, and along the lake shore, often integrated with urban green spaces. This makes the trails suitable for accessible strolls.

Q: Are there any circular or loop trails for easy hikes around Lac de Bordeaux?
A: Yes, many easy trails around Lac de Bordeaux are designed as loops, allowing you to start and end at the same point. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/10708073" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Bordeaux lake – Beach of Lake Bordeaux loop from Base Sous-Marine de Bordeaux&lt;/a&gt; is an easy 7.7 km route that offers varied scenery.

Q: What natural features or landmarks can I see on an easy hike around Lac de Bordeaux?
A: Easy hikes around Lac de Bordeaux offer views of the 1 km² artificial lake, surrounded by the Bois de Bordeaux woodland and expansive meadows. You can also explore the adjacent Parc Floral and experience the interconnected green spaces that link to natural reserves like the Barail reserve and the Réserve naturelle des Marais de Bruges.

Q: Are the easy hiking trails around Lac de Bordeaux suitable for families with children?
A: Absolutely. Lac de Bordeaux is a very family-friendly destination. The flat, accessible paths are ideal for walks with children, and the area offers additional amenities like supervised swimming areas, playgrounds, and even a mini-farm in Parc Bordelais, making it perfect for a day out with the family.

Q: Can I bring my dog on the easy hiking trails around Lac de Bordeaux?
A: Yes, many of the trails around Lac de Bordeaux are dog-friendly. It's generally recommended to keep your dog on a leash, especially in more populated areas or near wildlife reserves, to ensure a pleasant experience for everyone.

Q: What do other hikers enjoy most about the easy trails in Lac de Bordeaux?
A: The area is highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.4 stars from nearly 700 reviews. Hikers often praise the peaceful lakeside paths, the accessibility of the trails, and the pleasant mix of natural woodlands and open green spaces so close to the city.

Q: How long do easy hikes around Lac de Bordeaux typically take?
A: Easy hikes around Lac de Bordeaux vary in length and duration. Many popular routes can be completed in under two hours. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/41704312" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Bordeaux Lake loop from Réserve naturelle des Marais de Bruges&lt;/a&gt; is an easy 6.4 km trail that typically takes about 1 hour and 37 minutes.

Q: Is there parking available near the easy hiking trails at Lac de Bordeaux?
A: Yes, there are several parking options around Lac de Bordeaux, making it convenient to access the hiking trails. Specific parking areas are usually available near major access points to the lake and its surrounding parks.

Q: Are there any cafes or restaurants near the easy hiking trails for a break?
A: Yes, the area around Lac de Bordeaux is well-equipped with amenities. You'll find various dining options and cafes near the lake, particularly around the main recreational areas and the Plage du Lac, perfect for a break before or after your hike.

Q: What is the best time of year to go on an easy hike around Lac de Bordeaux?
A: Lac de Bordeaux is enjoyable year-round, but spring and autumn offer particularly pleasant conditions for easy hikes with mild temperatures and beautiful scenery. Summer is also popular, especially if you plan to combine your hike with swimming or other water activities at the lake's beach.

Q: Are there any viewpoints or scenic spots on the easy trails?
A: The easy trails around Lac de Bordeaux offer numerous scenic spots, primarily along the lake shore with expansive views across the water. The blend of woodlands, meadows, and the Parc Floral also provides picturesque settings for leisurely walks.
tips: Bordeaux Lake is one of the city's main swimming spots. It is a perfect place for families, walks or sports activities. The opportunity to cool off in the middle of a hike!

The Barail ecological reserve includes several sectors which share one thing in common: respect for nature and the preservation of a green site. Rich in biodiversity, the reserve can be visited all year round. Check the timetables here: https://www.bordeaux.fr/l853.

faqs.question: What is the difficulty level of the Super Balade 4: Tour and Detours at Bordeaux Lake trail?
faqs.answer: The Super Balade 4 trail is consistently rated as easy. It's designed as a short family walk, making it suitable for all ages and those looking for a gentle outdoor activity without a strenuous climb.

faqs.question: What kind of terrain can I expect on the Super Balade 4 trail?
faqs.answer: The trail offers a varied landscape, blending natural spaces with contemporary urban developments. You'll encounter a mix of natural areas, woodlands (including the 87-hectare Bordeaux wood with meadows and ponds), and modern urban settings. Much of the route likely follows paved or well-maintained paths, suitable for both walking and cycling.

faqs.question: What points of interest will I see along the Super Balade 4: Tour and Detours at Bordeaux Lake?
faqs.answer: This trail offers a diverse experience. You'll pass by the Bordeaux lake, the Tasta district with its green belt, ponds, and canals, Bordeaux Lake beach, and the unique Villabois housing estate. The route also features the significant Parc Floral, known for its impressive collections of peonies, magnolias, and a large rose garden. You'll also see the Matmut Atlantique stadium and the eco-labeled Ginko district.

faqs.question: Is the Super Balade 4 trail suitable for families with children?
faqs.answer: Yes, the Super Balade 4 trail is specifically highlighted as a 'short family walk' and is considered very family-friendly due to its easy difficulty and minimal elevation gain. It offers diverse scenery that can keep children engaged.

faqs.question: Is the Super Balade 4: Tour and Detours at Bordeaux Lake trail dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: While the trail passes through various urban and natural areas, specific regulations for dogs are not detailed. It's generally advisable to keep dogs on a leash, especially in shared spaces, urban sections, and protected natural areas like the Barail reserve, to ensure safety and respect for wildlife and other visitors.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to hike the Super Balade 4 trail?
faqs.answer: The trail can be enjoyed year-round, but spring and autumn are often ideal for comfortable temperatures and vibrant scenery, especially when the Parc Floral is in bloom. Summer can be pleasant, but ensure you have sun protection, as some sections may be exposed.

faqs.question: Are there any permits or entrance fees required for the Super Balade 4 trail?
faqs.answer: No specific permits or entrance fees are mentioned as required for hiking the Super Balade 4 trail itself. It is generally accessible to the public.

faqs.question: What should I know about visiting the Barail reserve along the trail?
faqs.answer: The trail includes sections that pass through The Barail reserve. This is an ecological reserve, so visitors should adhere to general conservation principles: stay on marked paths, do not disturb wildlife, and take all trash with you. No specific entrance fees or permits are noted for accessing this part of the trail.

faqs.question: Where can I park to access the Super Balade 4 trail?
faqs.answer: Specific parking information for the Super Balade 4 trail is not provided. However, given its peri-urban nature and proximity to Bordeaux Lake and various facilities, it's likely that public parking options are available in the surrounding districts like Tasta or near the Parc Floral and Matmut Atlantique stadium. We recommend checking local parking availability before your visit.

faqs.question: Does the Super Balade 4 trail connect with other routes?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route also passes through sections of other official trails. These include 'Super Balade 4 - Tour et détours à Bordeaux Lac', 'Super Balade 3 - Paysages de Jalles', 'ReVE 3 - Bordeaux - Parempuyre', 'Le Tour de Gironde à Vélo - Étape 6 (Blaye - Bordeaux)', and 'Voie Verte Bordeaux - Lacanau Océan'.

faqs.question: Are there facilities like cafes or restrooms available near the trail?
faqs.answer: Given that the trail traverses both natural areas and modern urban developments, including the Ginko district and areas around Bordeaux Lake, it is highly probable that you will find cafes, restaurants, and public restrooms in the vicinity of the urban sections and points of interest like the Parc Floral or Bordeaux Lake beach.
